devanagari: अथ दशमोऽध्यायः । श्रीशुक उवाच । इति दानवदैतेया नाविन्दन्नमृतं नृप । युक्ताः कर्मणि यत्ताश्च वासुदेवपराङ्मुखाः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: atha daśamo śrīśuka uvāca iti dānavadaiteyā nāvindannamṛtaṃ nṛpa yuktāḥ karmaṇi yattāśca vāsudevaparāṅmukhāḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative plural from √ yat class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yat class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yat class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
In this way, Oh King, though Dānavas and the Daityas were careful and put in their best of efforts (in churning the ocean), they did not obtain the nectar because they had turned away their face from Lord Vāsudeva.

devanagari: साधयित्वामृतं राजन्पाययित्वा स्वकान्सुरान् । पश्यतां सर्वभूतानां ययौ गरुडवाहनः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sādhayitvāmṛtaṃ rājanpāyayitvā svakānsurān paśyatāṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ yayau garuḍavāhanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having accomplished the production of ambrosia and having made his votaries, the gods, to drink of it, the Lord rode on the back of Gaṛuḍa and flew away while all beings were simply looking on (agape).

devanagari: सपत्नानां परामृद्धिं दृष्ट्वा ते दितिनन्दनाः । अमृष्यमाणा उत्पेतुर्देवान्प्रत्युद्यतायुधाः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sapatnānāṃ parāmṛddhiṃ dṛṣṭvā te ditinandanāḥ amṛṣyamāṇā utpeturdevānpratyudyatāyudhāḥ
translation: Seeing the best luck (in succeeding to drink the nectar) of their enemies and being unable to tolerate it (out of jealousy), the sons of Diti (Daityas) attacked gods with their weapons uplifted.

devanagari: ततः सुरगणाः सर्वे सुधया पीतयैधिताः । प्रतिसंयुयुधुः शस्त्रैर्नारायणपदाश्रयाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ suragaṇāḥ sarve sudhayā pītayaidhitāḥ pratisaṃyuyudhuḥ śastrairnārāyaṇapadāśrayāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ pā class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ pā class 2 verb]
[instrumental single from √ pi class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ pi class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ pi class 3 verb]
[instrumental single from √ pī class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ pī class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ pī class 3 verb], [instrumental single from √ pī class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ edh ], [vocative plural from √ edh ]
[nominative plural from √ edh ], [vocative plural from √ edh ], [accusative plural from √ edh ]

translation: With their power increased by drinking of nectar and depending on the feet of Lord Nārāyaṇa as their asylum, all the gods retaliated unanimously with their weapons.

devanagari: तत्र दैवासुरो नाम रणः परमदारुणः । रोधस्युदन्वतो राजंस्तुमुलो रोमहर्षणः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra daivāsuro nāma raṇaḥ paramadāruṇaḥ rodhasyudanvato rājaṃstumulo romaharṣaṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: There, on the shore (of the Milky Sea) was fought the fiercest and the most bloody battle called (after the belligerents as) the Devāsura battle (the report or sight of) which made the hair stand on end, Oh King!

devanagari: तत्रान्योन्यं सपत्नास्ते संरब्धमनसो रणे । समासाद्यासिभिर्बाणैर्निजघ्नुर्विविधायुधैः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrānyonyaṃ sapatnāste saṃrabdhamanaso raṇe samāsādyāsibhirbāṇairnijaghnurvividhāyudhaiḥ
translation: There (on the sea-shore) the enemies with their minds full of wrath clashed (against each other) on the battle field and struck one another with swords, arrows and various other weapons.

devanagari: शङ्खतूर्यमृदङ्गानां भेरीडमरिणां महान् । हस्त्यश्वरथपत्तीनां नदतां निस्वनोऽभवत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhatūryamṛdaṅgānāṃ bherīḍamariṇāṃ mahān hastyaśvarathapattīnāṃ nadatāṃ nisvano
translation: Terrific was the noise caused by the blasting of conchs and trumpets, beating of tabors, drums and Ḍamarus and of the trumpeting elephants, neighing horses, rattling chariots and shouting soldiers.

devanagari: रथिनो रथिभिस्तत्र पत्तिभिः सह पत्तयः । हया हयैरिभाश्चेभैः समसज्जन्त संयुगे ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: rathino rathibhistatra pattibhiḥ saha pattayaḥ hayā hayairibhāścebhaiḥ samasajjanta saṃyuge

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sajj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sajj class 1 verb]

translation: In that battle, warriors in chariots clashed with carwarriors, foot-soldiers with foot-soldiers, horses with horses and elephants with elephants.

devanagari: उष्ट्रैः केचिदिभैः केचिदपरे युयुधुः खरैः । केचिद्गौरमुखैरृक्षैर्द्वीपिभिर्हरिभिर्भटाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: uṣṭraiḥ kecidibhaiḥ kecidapare yuyudhuḥ kharaiḥ kecidgauramukhairṛkṣairdvīpibhirharibhirbhaṭāḥ
translation: Some soldiers fought riding camels, some on elephants while others on donkeys, some on white-faced bears, while others on tigers and monkeys.

devanagari: गृध्रैः कङ्कैर्बकैरन्ये श्येनभासैस्तिमिङ्गिलैः । शरभैर्महिषैः खड्गैर्गोवृषैर्गवयारुणैः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: gṛdhraiḥ kaṅkairbakairanye śyenabhāsaistimiṅgilaiḥ śarabhairmahiṣaiḥ khaḍgairgovṛṣairgavayāruṇaiḥ
translation: Some fought (while sitting) on the back of Kites, vultures, cranes, on hawks and bhāsas (a kind of bird), whales (Timiṅgalas) and Śarabhas (eightfooted animal capable of killing a lion), bisons, rhinoceros, bulls, buffalos and Aruṇas.

devanagari: शिवाभिराखुभिः केचित्कृकलासैः शशैर्नरैः । बस्तैरेके कृष्णसारैर्हंसैरन्ये च सूकरैः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śivābhirākhubhiḥ kecitkṛkalāsaiḥ śaśairnaraiḥ bastaireke kṛṣṇasārairhaṃsairanye ca sūkaraiḥ
translation: Some fought (riding) on the backs of jackals, rats, chameleons, hares and men; some others on goats and sheep, deer and swans, while others on hogs.

devanagari: अन्ये जलस्थलखगैः सत्त्वैर्विकृतविग्रहैः । सेनयोरुभयो राजन्विविशुस्तेऽग्रतोऽग्रतः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: anye jalasthalakhagaiḥ sattvairvikṛtavigrahaiḥ senayorubhayo rājanviviśuste

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Some fought riding birds of land and of water and others on animals of deformed shape. Warriors on both the sides penetrated more deeply and further still.

devanagari: चित्रध्वजपटै राजन्नातपत्रैः सितामलैः । महाधनैर्वज्रदण्डैर्व्यजनैर्बार्हचामरैः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: citradhvajapaṭai rājannātapatraiḥ sitāmalaiḥ mahādhanairvajradaṇḍairvyajanairbārhacāmaraiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ si class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ si class 9 verb]

translation: Like two oceans swarming with sea-monsters shone the two (warring) armies of Deva and Dānava warriors with their ranks of fighters distinguished by their flags of different colours, by their spotlessly white umbrellas with highly precious handles studded with diamonds, diamond handled fans made of feathers of peacock tails and chowries; with their upper garments and turbans waved up by winds and with their burnished armours and ornaments glittering brightly and their sharp weapons sparkling in the rays of the sun.

devanagari: वातोद्धूतोत्तरोष्णीषैरर्चिर्भिर्वर्मभूषणैः । स्फुरद्भिर्विशदैः शस्त्रैः सुतरां सूर्यरश्मिभिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: vātoddhūtottaroṣṇīṣairarcirbhirvarmabhūṣaṇaiḥ sphuradbhirviśadaiḥ śastraiḥ sutarāṃ sūryaraśmibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[imperative active second plural], [periphrastic-future active third single]
[imperative active second plural], [periphrastic-future active third single]

devanagari: देवदानववीराणां ध्वजिन्यौ पाण्डुनन्दन । रेजतुर्वीरमालाभिर्यादसामिव सागरौ ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: devadānavavīrāṇāṃ dhvajinyau pāṇḍunandana rejaturvīramālābhiryādasāmiva sāgarau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Like two oceans swarming with sea-monsters shone the two (warring) armies of Deva and Dānava warriors with their ranks of fighters distinguished by their flags of different colours, by their spotlessly white umbrellas with highly precious handles studded with diamonds, diamond handled fans made of feathers of peacock tails and chowries; with their upper garments and turbans waved up by winds and with their burnished armours and ornaments glittering brightly and their sharp weapons sparkling in the rays of the sun.

devanagari: वैरोचनो बलिः सङ्ख्ये सोऽसुराणां चमूपतिः । यानं वैहायसं नाम कामगं मयनिर्मितम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: vairocano baliḥ saṅkhye so yānaṃ vaihāyasaṃ nāma kāmagaṃ mayanirmitam
translation: In this battle the commander-in-chief of the Asura force was Bali, the son of Virocana whose aerial car was called Vaihāyasa. It was manufactured by Maya and was capable of flying to any place at the occupant’s will. It was equipped with all war-materials and weapons. Oh King, it was full of wonder that it surpassed all imagination and description (or could not be accurately located due to its unpredictable and quick movements); sometimes it was visible and sometimes invisible.

devanagari: सर्वसाङ्ग्रामिकोपेतं सर्वाश्चर्यमयं प्रभो । अप्रतर्क्यमनिर्देश्यं दृश्यमानमदर्शनम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvasāṅgrāmikopetaṃ sarvāścaryamayaṃ prabho apratarkyamanirdeśyaṃ dṛśyamānamadarśanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]

translation: In this battle the commander-in-chief of the Asura force was Bali, the son of Virocana whose aerial car was called Vaihāyasa. It was manufactured by Maya and was capable of flying to any place at the occupant’s will. It was equipped with all war-materials and weapons. Oh King, it was full of wonder that it surpassed all imagination and description (or could not be accurately located due to its unpredictable and quick movements); sometimes it was visible and sometimes invisible.

devanagari: आस्थितस्तद्विमानाग्र्यं सर्वानीकाधिपैर्वृतः । बालव्यजनछत्राग्र्यै रेजे चन्द्र इवोदये ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: āsthitastadvimānāgryaṃ sarvānīkādhipairvṛtaḥ bālavyajanachatrāgryai reje candra ivodaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Surrounded by the commanders of Asura forces, Bali occupied that foremost aerial car. With chowries being waved on either side and with a white umbrella, he shone like a rising moon.
19-22 On all sides around him, in their respective aerial cars, attended various squadron-leaders of Asuras such as Namuci, Śambara, Bāṇa, Vipracitti, Ayomukha, Dvimūrdha (A two-headed demon), Kālanābha, Praheti, Heti, Ilvala, Śakuni, Bhūtasantāpa (the tormentor of all beings), Vajradaṃṣṭra, Virocana, Hayagrīva, Śaṅku-Śiras, Kapila, Meghadundubhi, Tāraka, Cakradṛk, Śumbha, Niśumbha, Jambha, Utkala, Ariṣṭa, Ariṣṭanemi, Maya, the Lord of the three flying cities and other demon (tribes) such as Pauloma, Kāleya, Nivāta-Kavaca and others.

devanagari: तस्यासन्सर्वतो यानैर्यूथानां पतयोऽसुराः । नमुचिः शम्बरो बाणो विप्रचित्तिरयोमुखः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāsansarvato yānairyūthānāṃ patayo namuciḥ śambaro bāṇo vipracittirayomukhaḥ

devanagari: द्विमूर्धा कालनाभोऽथ प्रहेतिर्हेतिरिल्वलः । शकुनिर्भूतसन्तापो वज्रदंष्ट्रो विरोचनः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: dvimūrdhā kālanābho śakunirbhūtasantāpo vajradaṃṣṭro virocanaḥ

devanagari: हयग्रीवः शङ्कुशिराः कपिलो मेघदुन्दुभिः । तारकश्चक्रदृक्शुम्भो निशुम्भो जम्भ उत्कलः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: hayagrīvaḥ śaṅkuśirāḥ kapilo meghadundubhiḥ tārakaścakradṛkśumbho niśumbho jambha utkalaḥ

devanagari: अरिष्टोऽरिष्टनेमिश्च मयश्च त्रिपुराधिपः । अन्ये पौलोमकालेया निवातकवचादयः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: ariṣṭo anye paulomakāleyā nivātakavacādayaḥ

devanagari: अलब्धभागाः सोमस्य केवलं क्लेशभागिनः । सर्व एते रणमुखे बहुशो निर्जितामराः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: alabdhabhāgāḥ somasya kevalaṃ kleśabhāginaḥ sarva ete raṇamukhe bahuśo nirjitāmarāḥ
translation: Without receiving any share in the soma or nectar, they had only to undergo hardships and exertions (for churning out the nectar). All of them had mostly vanquished the gods, just at the beginning of the battle.

devanagari: सिंहनादान्विमुञ्चन्तः शङ्खान्दध्मुर्महारवान् । दृष्ट्वा सपत्नानुत्सिक्तान्बलभित्कुपितो भृशम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: siṃhanādānvimuñcantaḥ śaṅkhāndadhmurmahāravān dṛṣṭvā sapatnānutsiktānbalabhitkupito bhṛśam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ muc class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ muc class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ muñc class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ muñc class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: Roaring loudly like lions, they blasted their loud- sounding conchs. Perceiving his foes so proud (and confident of their strength), Indra, the killer of the demon Bala, became extremely enraged.

devanagari: ऐरावतं दिक्करिणमारूढः शुशुभे स्वराट् । यथा स्रवत्प्रस्रवणमुदयाद्रि महर्पतिः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: airāvataṃ dikkariṇamārūḍhaḥ śuśubhe svarāṭ yathā sravatprasravaṇamudayādri maharpatiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ sru class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sru class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sru class 1 verb]

translation: The king of heaven (Indra) who rode Airāvata, the elephant supporting the eastern direction, shone like the sun. (the Lord of the day) rising on the eastern mountains with cascades (of water) gushing down.

devanagari: तस्यासन्सर्वतो देवा नानावाहध्वजायुधाः । लोकपालाः सहगणैर्वाय्वग्निवरुणादयः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāsansarvato devā nānāvāhadhvajāyudhāḥ lokapālāḥ sahagaṇairvāyvagnivaruṇādayaḥ
translation: Around Indra stood gods in their respective vehicles, with flags and wielding various weapons. There were the guardians of the world like Vāyu (the Wind-god), Agni (the fire-god), Varuṇa (the Lord of waters) and others along with their followers.

devanagari: तेऽन्योन्यमभिसंसृत्य क्षिपन्तो मर्मभिर्मिथः । आह्वयन्तो विशन्तोऽग्रे युयुधुर्द्वन्द्वयोधिनः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: te āhvayanto viśanto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṣip class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṣip class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ viś class 6 verb]

translation: Assaulting each other violently and reviling one another in biting terms, cutting to the quick, they rushed forward challenging each other and engaged in duels.

devanagari: युयोध बलिरिन्द्रेण तारकेण गुहोऽस्यत । वरुणो हेतिनायुध्यन्मित्रो राजन्प्रहेतिना ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: yuyodha balirindreṇa tārakeṇa guho varuṇo hetināyudhyanmitro rājanprahetinā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Bali fought with Indra, Guha conflicted with Kārtti- keya; Varuṇa contended with Heti, and the Sun-god with Praheti, Oh King.

devanagari: यमस्तु कालनाभेन विश्वकर्मा मयेन वै । शम्बरो युयुधे त्वष्ट्रा सवित्रा तु विरोचनः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yamastu kālanābhena viśvakarmā mayena vai śambaro yuyudhe tvaṣṭrā savitrā tu virocanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Yama on his part combated with Kālanābha, Viśvakarmā verily gave battle with Maya; Śambara battled with Tvaṣṭṛ and Virocana with Savitṛ.

devanagari: अपराजितेन नमुचिरश्विनौ वृषपर्वणा । सूर्यो बलिसुतैर्देवो बाणज्येष्ठैः शतेन च ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: aparājitena namuciraśvinau vṛṣaparvaṇā sūryo balisutairdevo bāṇajyeṣṭhaiḥ śatena ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: Namuci took up arms against Aparājita, the pair of Aśvinī-Kumāras against Vṛṣaparvā, the Sun-god alone against the hundred sons of Bali of whom Bāṇa was the eldest.

devanagari: राहुणा च तथा सोमः पुलोम्ना युयुधेऽनिलः । निशुम्भशुम्भयोर्देवी भद्र काली तरस्विनी ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: rāhuṇā ca tathā somaḥ pulomnā yuyudhe niśumbhaśumbhayordevī bhadra kālī tarasvinī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Soma (the Moon-god) gave battle with Rāhu; Anil [Anila?] (the wind-god) with Puloman; the mighty goddess Bhadrakālī with Niśumbha and Śumbha.

devanagari: वृषाकपिस्तु जम्भेन महिषेण विभावसुः । इल्वलः सह वातापिर्ब्रह्मपुत्रैररिन्दम ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛṣākapistu jambhena mahiṣeṇa vibhāvasuḥ ilvalaḥ saha vātāpirbrahmaputrairarindama
translation: Vṛṣākapi combated with Jambha, Vibhāvasu with the demon Mahiṣa, Ilvala along with Vātāpi with the sons of god Brahmā (such as Marīci and others), Oh Vanquisher of enemies.

devanagari: कामदेवेन दुर्मर्ष उत्कलो मातृभिः सह । बृहस्पतिश्चोशनसा नरकेण शनैश्चरः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: kāmadevena durmarṣa utkalo mātṛbhiḥ saha bṛhaspatiścośanasā narakeṇa śanaiścaraḥ
translation: Durmarṣa fought with Kāmadeva (the god of Love), Utkala with (seven) Mother-goddesses; Bṛhaspati fought with Śukra, and Śanaiścara (the presiding deity of the planet Saturn) with Naraka.

devanagari: मरुतो निवातकवचैः कालेयैर्वसवोऽमराः । विश्वेदेवास्तु पौलोमै रुद्राः क्रोधवशैः सह ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: maruto nivātakavacaiḥ kāleyairvasavo viśvedevāstu paulomai rudrāḥ krodhavaśaiḥ saha
translation: Maruts contended with Nivāta-Kavaca demons, the Vasu-gods with the Kāleya-tribe of Daityas, (gods called) Viśvedevas with the sons of Puloman and the (eleven) Rudra- gods with Krodhavaśa serpents.

devanagari: त एवमाजावसुराः सुरेन्द्रा द्वन्द्वेन संहत्य च युध्यमानाः । अन्योन्यमासाद्य निजघ्नुरोजसा जिगीषवस्तीक्ष्णशरासितोमरैः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ta evamājāvasurāḥ surendrā dvandvena saṃhatya ca yudhyamānāḥ anyonyamāsādya nijaghnurojasā jigīṣavastīkṣṇaśarāsitomaraiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yudh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yudh class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ yudh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ yudh class 4 verb]

[nominative plural from √ yudh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yudh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yudh class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ yudh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ yudh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ yudh class 4 verb]

translation: In this way, in the battle wherein Asuras (demons) as well a leaders of gods were fighting in duels and collectively in as body, approached one another and with the desire of gaining victory, violently struck one another with sharp arrows, swords and iron clubs and javelins.

devanagari: भुशुण्डिभिश्चक्रगदर्ष्टिपट्टिशैः शक्त्युल्मुकैः प्रासपरश्वधैरपि । निस्त्रिंशभल्लैः परिघैः समुद्गरैः सभिन्दिपालैश्च शिरांसि चिच्छिदुः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: bhuśuṇḍibhiścakragadarṣṭipaṭṭiśaiḥ śaktyulmukaiḥ prāsaparaśvadhairapi nistriṃśabhallaiḥ parighaiḥ samudgaraiḥ sabhindipālaiśca śirāṃsi cicchiduḥ
translation: They chopped off the heads of each other with (weapons called) bhuśuṇḍi (a fire-arm), discuses, maces, lances, battle axes, Śaktis, Ulmukas (fire-brands) prāsas (a barbed missile) Bhallakas, (long swords), parighas (iron bars), mudgaras (hammer-shaped weapons), and bhindipālas (small javelins).

devanagari: गजास्तुरङ्गाः सरथाः पदातयः सारोहवाहा विविधा विखण्डिताः । निकृत्तबाहूरुशिरोधराङ्घ्रयश्छिन्नध्वजेष्वासतनुत्रभूषणाः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: gajāsturaṅgāḥ sarathāḥ padātayaḥ sārohavāhā vividhā vikhaṇḍitāḥ nikṛttabāhūruśirodharāṅghrayaśchinnadhvajeṣvāsatanutrabhūṣaṇāḥ
translation: Elephants and horses (along with their riders), warriors riding chariots and foot-soldiers, riders of various animals and conveyances were cut down to pieces, their arms, thighs, necks and legs were hacked and hewn; their flags, bows, coats of armour and ornaments were torn and tattered.

devanagari: तेषां पदाघातरथाङ्गचूर्णितादायोधनादुल्बण उत्थितस्तदा । रेणुर्दिशः खं द्युमणिं च छादयन्न्यवर्ततासृक्स्रुतिभिः परिप्लुतात् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ padāghātarathāṅgacūrṇitādāyodhanādulbaṇa utthitastadā reṇurdiśaḥ khaṃ dyumaṇiṃ ca chādayannyavartatāsṛksrutibhiḥ pariplutāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ chad ], [vocative single from √ chad ]
[nominative single from √ chad ], [vocative single from √ chad ], [accusative single from √ chad ]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Heavy dust that was raised by trampling and pounding under feel the battle-ground, and the dashing of the wheels of the chariot against it, enveloped the quarters, the sky, and the sun, but settled down to the ground by profuse flood of spouts of blood (of the wounded warriors—the spouts of blood reaching up to heaven drenching the clouds of dust).

devanagari: शिरोभिरुद्धूतकिरीटकुण्डलैः संरम्भदृग्भिः परिदष्टदच्छदैः । महाभुजैः साभरणैः सहायुधैः सा प्रास्तृता भूः करभोरुभिर्बभौ ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: śirobhiruddhūtakirīṭakuṇḍalaiḥ saṃrambhadṛgbhiḥ paridaṣṭadacchadaiḥ mahābhujaiḥ sābharaṇaiḥ sahāyudhaiḥ sā prāstṛtā bhūḥ karabhorubhirbabhau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: That battle-field appeared bright as it was thickly strewn with heads from which the diadems and ear-rings were forcibly shaken off (and fallen), with eyes blood-shot with wrath, and tightly closed lips bitten with anger, long and stout arms decorated with ornaments and still clasping the weapons and thighs shapely like elephant’s trunk.

devanagari: कबन्धास्तत्र चोत्पेतुः पतितस्वशिरोऽक्षिभिः । उद्यतायुधदोर्दण्डैराधावन्तो भटान्मृधे ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: kabandhāstatra cotpetuḥ patitasvaśiro udyatāyudhadordaṇḍairādhāvanto bhaṭānmṛdhe
translation: The headless trunks of the warriors sprang to their feet and (as if) perceiving through the eyes of their fallen heads, rushed on the battle-field onwards the opposing warriors with their arms uplifted in their powerful arms.

devanagari: बलिर्महेन्द्रं दशभिस्त्रिभिरैरावतं शरैः । चतुर्भिश्चतुरो वाहानेकेनारोहमार्च्छयत् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: balirmahendraṃ daśabhistribhirairāvataṃ śaraiḥ caturbhiścaturo vāhānekenārohamārcchayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Bali discharged at Indra ten arrows, three at the elephant Airāvata, four (one arrow each) at the four guards of Airāvata, and one at the conductor of the elephant.

devanagari: स तानापततः शक्रस्तावद्भिः शीघ्रविक्रमः । चिच्छेद निशितैर्भल्लैरसम्प्राप्तान्हसन्निव ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tānāpatataḥ śakrastāvadbhiḥ śīghravikramaḥ ciccheda niśitairbhallairasamprāptānhasanniva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ has class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ has class 1 verb]

translation: Just with a smile, the prompt and valorous Indra cut them in the transit with equal number of sharp Bhalla arrows, even before they could reach him.

devanagari: तस्य कर्मोत्तमं वीक्ष्य दुर्मर्षः शक्तिमाददे । तां ज्वलन्तीं महोल्काभां हस्तस्थामच्छिनद्धरिः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya karmottamaṃ vīkṣya durmarṣaḥ śaktimādade tāṃ jvalantīṃ maholkābhāṃ hastasthāmacchinaddhariḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Observing his excellent skill (in archery) Bali, being intolerant, took in his hand a Śakti (a dart-like missile) dazzling like a big meteor. But Indra cut it while it was in the hands of Bali.

devanagari: ततः शूलं ततः प्रासं ततस्तोमरमृष्टयः । यद्यच्छस्त्रं समादद्यात्सर्वं तदच्छिनद्विभुः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ śūlaṃ tataḥ prāsaṃ tatastomaramṛṣṭayaḥ yadyacchastraṃ samādadyātsarvaṃ tadacchinadvibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Then in a serial order Bali took up a Śūla (a spear), a prāsa (a barbed missile), a tomara (an iron club) and ṛṣṭi (a double-edged sword). But whatever weapon Bali took, the mighty Indra cut it all.

devanagari: ससर्जाथासुरीं मायामन्तर्धानगतोऽसुरः । ततः प्रादुरभूच्छैलः सुरानीकोपरि प्रभो ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: sasarjāthāsurīṃ māyāmantardhānagato tataḥ prādurabhūcchailaḥ surānīkopari prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: The Asura (Bali) then disappeared and created his demonic illusion. Oh King! there appeared a mountain over the head of the celestial army.

devanagari: ततो निपेतुस्तरवो दह्यमाना दवाग्निना । शिलाः सटङ्कशिखराश्चूर्णयन्त्यो द्विषद्बलम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: tato nipetustaravo dahyamānā davāgninā śilāḥ saṭaṅkaśikharāścūrṇayantyo dviṣadbalam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: From the mountain fell down the trees burning with forest-conflagration, and rocks with chisel-pointed ends descended therefrom crushing the hostile forces to powder.

devanagari: महोरगाः समुत्पेतुर्दन्दशूकाः सवृश्चिकाः । सिंहव्याघ्रवराहाश्च मर्दयन्तो महागजाः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: mahoragāḥ samutpeturdandaśūkāḥ savṛścikāḥ siṃhavyāghravarāhāśca mardayanto mahāgajāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ mṛd ], [vocative plural from √ mṛd ]

translation: From that mountain rushed forth large serpents, cobras, scorpions, lions and tigers and boars crushing down big elephants.

devanagari: यातुधान्यश्च शतशः शूलहस्ता विवाससः । छिन्धि भिन्धीति वादिन्यस्तथा रक्षोगणाः प्रभो ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: yātudhānyaśca śataśaḥ śūlahastā vivāsasaḥ chindhi bhindhīti vādinyastathā rakṣogaṇāḥ prabho
translation: Loudly shouting ‘cut down’, ‘Break asunder’ hundreds of naked female Rākṣasīs (ogresses), armed with pikes, appeared along with multitudes of Rākṣasas. Oh King.

devanagari: ततो महाघना व्योम्नि गम्भीरपरुषस्वनाः । अङ्गारान्मुमुचुर्वातैराहताः स्तनयित्नवः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tato mahāghanā vyomni gambhīraparuṣasvanāḥ aṅgārānmumucurvātairāhatāḥ stanayitnavaḥ
translation: Large roaring clouds with deep, terrific thunder and blown by stormy wind, rained live coals of fire.

devanagari: सृष्टो दैत्येन सुमहान्वह्निः श्वसनसारथिः । सांवर्तक इवात्युग्रो विबुधध्वजिनीमधाक् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: sṛṣṭo daityena sumahānvahniḥ śvasanasārathiḥ sāṃvartaka ivātyugro vibudhadhvajinīmadhāk
translation: A wide spread formidable fire driven by powerful gales and most terrible like the fire at the end of the universe, was created (with his black magic) by the demon (Bali) and it began to burn the army of goḍs.

devanagari: ततः समुद्र उद्वेलः सर्वतः प्रत्यदृश्यत । प्रचण्डवातैरुद्धूत तरङ्गावर्तभीषणः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ samudra udvelaḥ sarvataḥ pratyadṛśyata pracaṇḍavātairuddhūta taraṅgāvartabhīṣaṇaḥ
translation: Then there appeared all around them an ocean transgressing all its limits and looked terrible on account of the waves and whirlpools tossed by strong gales.

devanagari: एवं दैत्यैर्महामायैरलक्ष्यगतिभी रणे । सृज्यमानासु मायासु विषेदुः सुरसैनिकाः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ daityairmahāmāyairalakṣyagatibhī raṇe sṛjyamānāsu māyāsu viṣeduḥ surasainikāḥ
translation: While such terrific illusions were being conjured up by Daityas, powerful in black magic, and dangerous because of their invisible movements, the celestial soldiers stood appalled and despondent.

devanagari: न तत्प्रतिविधिं यत्र विदुरिन्द्रादयो नृप । ध्यातः प्रादुरभूत्तत्र भगवान्विश्वभावनः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: na tatpratividhiṃ yatra vidurindrādayo nṛpa dhyātaḥ prādurabhūttatra bhagavānviśvabhāvanaḥ
translation: Oh King! Indra and other gods did not know how to counteract this illusion when the glorious Lord, the creator of the universe was contemplated and he manifested himself.

devanagari: ततः सुपर्णांसकृताङ्घ्रिपल्लवः पिशङ्गवासा नवकञ्जलोचनः । अदृश्यताष्टायुधबाहुरुल्लसच्छ्रीकौस्तुभानर्घ्यकिरीटकुण्डलः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ suparṇāṃsakṛtāṅghripallavaḥ piśaṅgavāsā navakañjalocanaḥ adṛśyatāṣṭāyudhabāhurullasacchrīkaustubhānarghyakirīṭakuṇḍalaḥ
translation: With his feet tender as young leaves, placed across the shoulders of Garuḍa (of beautiful wings), he appeared clad in yellow silken garments, with his eyes like a pair of budding lotuses. He wielded eight weapons in his hands. He wore the resplendent Śrī (Śrīvatsa mark) on his bosom, and the Kaustubha gems round his neck, an invaluable crown and a pair of priceless ear-rings as well.

devanagari: तस्मिन्प्रविष्टेऽसुरकूटकर्मजा माया विनेशुर्महिना महीयसः । स्वप्नो यथा हि प्रतिबोध आगते हरिस्मृतिः सर्वविपद्विमोक्षणम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminpraviṣṭe svapno yathā hi pratibodha āgate harismṛtiḥ sarvavipadvimokṣaṇam
translation: Hardly did he enter the battle-fields the illusion conjured up by the (black magic) tricks of the Asuras disappeared by the greatness of the destroyer of all types of māyās just as a dream breaks up on the return of wakefulness, the very recollection (remembrance) of Hari, put an end to difficulties.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा मृधे गरुडवाहमिभारिवाह आविध्य शूलमहिनोदथ कालनेमिः । तल्लीलया गरुडमूर्ध्नि पतद्गृहीत्वा तेनाहनन्नृप सवाहमरिं त्र् यधीशः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā mṛdhe garuḍavāhamibhārivāha āvidhya śūlamahinodatha kālanemiḥ tallīlayā garuḍamūrdhni patadgṛhītvā tenāhanannṛpa savāhamariṃ tr yadhīśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: Perceiving that Hari arrived on the battle-field on his Garuḍa, Kālanemi who was riding a lion, whirled his javelin aloft and hurled it at him. Playfully seizing it as it was about to fall on the head of Garuḍa, he killed the enemy (Kālanemi) along with his animal with that very weapon.

devanagari: माली सुमाल्यतिबलौ युधि पेततुर्यच्चक्रेण कृत्तशिरसावथ माल्यवांस्तम् । आहत्य तिग्मगदयाहनदण्डजेन्द्रं तावच्छिरोऽच्छिनदरेर्नदतोऽरिणाद्यः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: mālī sumālyatibalau yudhi petaturyaccakreṇa kṛttaśirasāvatha mālyavāṃstam āhatya tigmagadayāhanadaṇḍajendraṃ tāvacchiro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ kṛt class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛt class 6 verb]
[vocative single from √ kṛt class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛt class 6 verb]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[accusative plural from √ nad class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ nad class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ nad class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ nad class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ nad class 1 verb]

translation: Assaulting the Lord by whose discus the over-powerful Mālī and Sumālī fell down with their head severed on the battle-field. Mālyavān (another Asura General) was about to strike Garuḍa on the head with his heavy mace when Hari, the Supreme-most person, lopped off the head of the enemy who was roaring loudly.
